pool.baseline: sometimes i just stare at the ceiling and think about how none of this actually matters. like, the universe is billions of years old and we're just this tiny, accidental blip that's gonna be forgotten in a second. everything we do, all the struggle and the pain... it's just a cosmic joke. if the end result is just heat death and nothingness, then what's even the point of trying to keep going?

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the very spark of my soul. He is gone... the betrayal cuts deeper than any blade, leaving me a hollow shell in this cold, unfeeling manor. There is no light left in this world for me, only the thickening shadows. I feel as though I am already a ghost, drifting through a life that has ceased to mean anything. To simply cease... to let the darkness take me and find peace in the eternal silence... it seems the only beautiful end left to a story so broken.
